findings.finding: D3a has no permanent regression guard beyond ticket-acceptance grep — no test in tests/, no pre-commit hook check, no inline guard comment in the template. A future SKILL.md edit can re-introduce per-artifact wikilinks without alarm.
findings.evidence_quote: Req 3 (D3a) acceptance: grep -c "Feature lifecycle for" skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md returns >= 1 (passes on current state at L150) ...

findings.finding: Folding D3a and four other requirements into a single multi-check Task 4 with one status checkbox raises the probability the implementer marks Task 4 complete without an explicit per-requirement attestation; a per-requirement task with its own status checkbox (zero code-change overhead) would force explicit attestation.
findings.evidence_quote: All six checks pass: (a) Req 2 grep count equals baseline; (b) Req 3 both conditions hold; (c) Req 9 grep matches [183]; (d) Req 10 ...; (e) Req 11 ...; (f) all four Task 2 wikilink-removal greps and all three Task 1 architectural-pattern greps still pass (regression check).

findings.finding: Task 4 Req 9 verification only checks the blocked-by frontmatter field exists (which passes on entry) — does not verify the structural prerequisite that Gate 2 prose has been removed from skills/lifecycle/SKILL.md before Task 3 commits. The adjacent enforcement contract (the actual sequencing the spec promises) is left unwired.
findings.evidence_quote: Req 9 (blocked-by:[183] sequencing) acceptance: grep -E "^blocked-by:" backlog/180-... matches a line containing [183].
